title: Freakonomics
subtitle: A Rogue Economist Explores the Hidden Side of Everything
author: Steven D. Levitt, Stephen J. Dubner
themes: Incentives are the root of all behavior
Dramatic effects have distant, subtle causes
Data reveals the hidden side of everything
awards: Book Sense Book of the Year (Adult Nonfiction, 2006)
Quill Award for Best Business Book
Indies Choice Book Award for Adult Nonfiction
National Council on Economic Education Visionary Award
Over 4 million copies sold worldwide in 35+ languages
excerpt: What do schoolteachers and sumo wrestlers have in common? How is the Ku Klux Klan like a group of real estate agents? Why do drug dealers still live with their moms? Freakonomics uses data to reveal the hidden incentives that shape human behavior — and turns conventional wisdom on its head.
